metadata.title: Advance Payment Message Examples | FlowDockr
metadata.description: Deposit and advance-payment message examples for freelancers, with three distinct pre-kickoff strategies and a direct path into the unpaid deposit scenario.

hero.title: Advance payment message examples before kickoff
hero.description: This page handles one job only: pre-kickoff deposit and advance-payment wording. Use the guide for policy, use these examples for reusable phrasing, and move into the scenario when the client's exact hesitation needs a tailored response.

examples.title: Three strategically different pre-kickoff payment versions
examples.description: Each version protects the deposit boundary in a different way depending on the client's pressure or hesitation.

items.title: Standard kickoff confirmation
items.description: Example: "Once the deposit is in place, I can lock the kickoff and move forward right away. Let me know as soon as that step is completed on your side."

items.title: Trust-building deposit ask
items.description: Example: "Happy to answer any final questions before kickoff. Once the deposit is confirmed, I can reserve the start date and send over the next project steps."

items.title: Firm start-after-payment boundary
items.description: Example: "I start once the agreed deposit is in place so the timeline stays protected on both sides. As soon as that is confirmed, I can begin."

usage.label: When to use each
usage.title: Pick the version by pre-kickoff pressure
usage.description: Match the example to the real pre-kickoff issue instead of sending the same deposit line in every situation.

items.title: Use the standard version for normal kickoff
items.description: Best when the client is moving ahead and you just need to keep the deposit step explicit.

items.title: Use the trust-building version when they hesitate
items.description: Best when the client wants reassurance before sending the deposit but you do not want to add unpaid custom work.

items.title: Use the firm version when they want to start now
items.description: Best when the client treats the deposit like an optional step and you need to protect the payment boundary clearly.

bridge.title: When the example should become a tailored scenario reply
bridge.description: Templates work when the payment issue is still simple. Move into the scenario when the client's exact pushback needs a more specific response.

items.title: The deposit was promised but still has not arrived
items.description: Use the scenario when the issue is no longer hypothetical and kickoff is now blocked.

items.title: The client wants to start now and pay later
items.description: Use the scenario when you need wording that reacts to that exact boundary pressure.

items.title: You need to answer their specific hesitation
items.description: Use the scenario when reassurance, timing, and payment need to be balanced in one thread-specific reply.

cta.title: Move into the scenario when deposit wording needs judgment.
cta.description: These examples give you reusable pre-kickoff language. If the deposit was promised but still has not arrived, or the client is pushing the boundary, open the scenario page for a tailored reply.
